title: 10 best herbal essential oils for cough
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for cough are plant-derived extracts that provide natural relief from coughing. They help by loosening mucus, reducing inflammation, and soothing irritated throats. The best herbal essential oils for cough include Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Rosmarinus officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Hyptis suaveolens, and Pimpinella anisum. If left untreated, coughs can disrupt sleep, hinder daily activities, and exacerbate underlying health conditions, negatively impacting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Eucalyptus oil helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion associated with a cough.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling in the throat and airways, which can alleviate soreness and discomfort caused by coughing.
Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Eucalyptus oil has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial effects, helping to combat infections that may be contributing to a cough.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, essential oils helps with cough because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil's primary active compound, eucalyptol, breaks down mucus, making it easier to expel. This leads to rapid relief from congestion and coughs. Additionally, eucalyptus oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the airways, allowing for better airflow and a smoother breathing experience, providing temporary relief from coughs and congestion caused by colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (1,8-Cineole): This terpene acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.
α-Pinene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving cough and congestion.
Limonene: This terpene has a bronchodilatory effect, which means it helps to relax and widen the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughs.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a key compound responsible for their decongestant and expectorant properties.
Buds: Buds are also widely used as they contain a high concentration of eucalyptol and other volatile oils that help to relieve cough and congestion.
Stems: While less common, stems are sometimes used for essential oil production due to their lower cost and still significant eucalyptol content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a healthy plant.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Mix 50ml of high-proof vodka with 250g of chopped leaves in the jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, then strain and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Relieves congestion and eases coughing by helping to break down mucus and open up airways, making it easier to expel.
Menthol: Provides temporary pain relief and reduces inflammation in the throat, making it a natural remedy for soothing sore throats and reducing coughing spasms.
Rosmarinic acid: Has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and nasal passages, further alleviating coughing and congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, essential oils helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties. The oil's high menthol content helps thin mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel, thus providing relief from congestion. Additionally, its cooling and decongestant properties help to soothe the throat and calm the cough reflex. As a result, peppermint essential oil can be a valuable natural remedy for relieving coughs and other respiratory issues. Its invigorating scent also helps to reduce stress and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This monoterpenoid helps with cough by acting as a bronchodilator, thereby relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Menthol: This monoterpenoid helps with cough by providing local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce coughing spasms and discomfort in the throat.
Linalool: This terpenoid helps with cough by exhibiting expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which are key compounds responsible for the oil's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Stems: Stems are also widely used, as they contain a significant amount of menthol and other volatile compounds that help to soothe and calm the respiratory system, making them effective in relieving coughs and colds.

Flowers: Flowers are used to create a more subtle and delicate essential oil, which is often used to help alleviate coughs and congestion in a more gentle and soothing manner, while also providing a pleasant aroma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh leaves from mentha x piperita plants in the morning when oil content is highest.
Steam distill the leaves and flowers at 100-110°c for 1-2 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and particles.
Store the herbal mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause respiratory infections and congestion, which contribute to coughing.

Decongestant and expectorant properties: Thyme oil's decongestant properties help reduce mucus production and congestion in the chest and sinuses, while its expectorant properties loosen and clear out excess mucus, providing relief from a stubborn cough.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by coughing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, essential oils helps with cough because of its natural decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that help combat respiratory infections and reduce inflammation in the airways. As a result, thyme essential oil can help loosen and clear mucus, relieving cough and congestion, and promoting easier breathing. It's also known to soothe the throat and calm coughing spasms, providing relief from cough and cold symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: This monoterpene helps with cough by exhibiting expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways.
Thymol: This phenolic compound acts as an antitussive, helping to suppress coughing by reducing inflammation and relieving irritation in the respiratory tract.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate cough caused by respiratory infections by reducing the growth of bacteria and viruses in the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves from the Thymus vulgaris plant are commonly used to make essential oils for cough due to their high thymol content, which has decongestant and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make essential oils for cough relief because they contain thymol and other compounds that help soothe respiratory issues.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are often utilized in making essential oils for cough due to their rich content of thymol, which aids in reducing inflammation and congestion in the throat and lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from organic gardens at dawn when dew is still present.
Steam distill the leaves for 2 hours at 100 degrees celsius to extract essential oils slowly.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separatory funnel carefully.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid safely.
Label the bottle with the date and contents clearly and store it in a cool dry place.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Help to combat respiratory infections such as bronchitis and pneumonia, which often accompany a persistent cough.
Decongestant properties: Constrict blood vessels in the nose and sinuses, reducing swelling and congestion that can exacerbate coughing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviate irritating coughs.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, essential oils helps with cough because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have been shown to soothe irritated throat tissues and combat bacterial and viral infections. When inhaled or applied topically, tea tree oil can help ease coughs by reducing inflammation and congestion, providing quick relief from colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. Its expectorant properties also help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps with cough by acting as an expectorant, thinning mucus and making it easier to cough up.
Alpha-terpinene: It helps with cough by reducing inflammation in the airways and relieving congestion.
Viridiflorol: It helps with cough by displaying antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the presence of pathogens that may be contributing to the cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil due to their high concentration of cineole, which is known for its decongestant and expectorant properties.
Stems: Stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used to produce essential oil, which is rich in cineole and has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as cough and congestion.

Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia are another source of essential oil, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in soothing coughs and sore throats.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves of melaleuca alternifolia from mature plants in the wild or in a controlled environment.
Dry the leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 1 to 2 weeks.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids to obtain the herbal tea.

remedy_properties: Antitussive: English lavender essential oil has antitussive properties, which means it can help to relieve and reduce the frequency and severity of coughs.
Anti-inflammatory: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated throats, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with coughing.
Expectorant: English lavender essential oil has expectorant properties, which means it can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and expel congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, essential oils helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, providing quick and effective relief from coughs. Its soothing aroma also promotes relaxation, further alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene compound found in Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, linalool has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, and promote the clearance of mucus, thereby alleviating cough symptoms.

Linalyl acetate: A terpene ester present in Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, linalyl acetate has a soothing and calming effect on the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and inflammation, and thus providing relief from cough and congestion.

Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene compound found in Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, caryophyllene oxide has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, and also exhibit antimicrobial activity, preventing the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can contribute to cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high amounts of camphor, a compound that helps in reducing cough and congestion.
Flowers: Rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, the flowers provide a soothing and calming effect, which can help alleviate cough symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia contain a combination of camphor and linalool, making them a good source for essential oil production to treat cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when dew is present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place for 7 to 10 days with minimal disturbance.
Steam distill 500 grams of dried flowers in a still under low pressure for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil in a separate container as the distillation process yields 20 ml.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and achieve clarity.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Rosemary essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause cough, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can ease coughing and congestion symptoms.

Expectorant property: Rosemary essential oil is an expectorant that helps loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, essential oils helps with cough because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. It helps to relieve congestion in the chest and throat, making it easier to cough up mucus and phlegm. The oil's antimicrobial properties also help to combat infections that can cause coughs. Additionally, rosemary essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated airways, providing quick relief from coughs and congestion. This makes it a popular natural remedy for coughs and colds.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene present in Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil, it helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate cough symptoms by exerting anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties.
Camphene: A monoterpenic hydrocarbon found in the essential oil, it acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby relieving cough.

Bornyl acetate: A monoterpenic ester present in the essential oil, it possesses anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are commonly used to make essential oils for cough relief due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are also used to make essential oils for cough relief, as they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the respiratory tract.
Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are sometimes used to make essential oils for cough relief, as they contain oils that can help to ease congestion and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated to prevent contamination.
Clean and dry the leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris using a food dehydrator at 35-40 degrees celsius.
Steam distill the dried leaves for 2-3 hours to extract the oil using a copper still at 100-110 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separator or a centrifuge for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle for up to 2 years.

remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, a compound found in black pepper essential oil, has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.

Antimicrobial activity: Black pepper essential oil's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause coughs by targeting bacteria, viruses, and fungi that may be contributing to the condition.
Anti-inflammatory action: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can exacerbate coughing and make it more difficult to breathe.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum, also known as black pepper, essential oils helps with cough because of its expectorant properties. The oil contains compounds like piperine and beta-caryophyllene that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. These properties help in loosening and clearing mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties also aid in reducing the thickness of mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby providing relief from cough and other respiratory issues. This natural remedy offers a soothing solution for cough relief.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, thereby alleviating cough.
α-Pinene: As a terpene, α-pinene exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough out.
Germacrene D: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections that may be causing cough, such as bacterial or viral infections in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Piper nigrum seeds are often used for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving cough symptoms.
Leaves: Piper nigrum leaves are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe irritated throats and reduce coughing.
Fruits: Piper nigrum fruits, also known as peppercorns, contain compounds that have natural expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh piper nigrum berries in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe and fragrant.
Clean the berries thoroughly by washing them with water and drying them with a clean towel.
Steam distillation is used to extract the essential oil from the berries for 2-3 hours.
The distilled oil is then separated from the water using a separatory funnel for 30 minutes.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Purple coneflower essential oil contains compounds like beta-amyrin and betulinic acid, which have been shown to suppress cough reflexes and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory activity, which can help alleviate inflammation in the respiratory tract that contributes to cough symptoms, such as bronchitis and asthma.

Antiviral properties: Purple coneflower essential oil exhibits antiviral activity against viruses like influenza and herpes simplex, which are common causes of coughs; this property helps to reduce the severity and duration of viral-induced coughs.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, essential oils helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with coughs. By promoting a healthy immune response and protecting against infections, Echinacea purpurea essential oils can help alleviate cough symptoms and support overall respiratory well-being. Its decongestant properties also help ease congestion and promote easier breathing.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the severity and frequency of coughing.
Cichorin: A phenolic compound with expectorant properties, which can help to thin and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.
Iridoid glycosides: A type of terpene that can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and airways, making it less uncomfortable to cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds that have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and fight off infections.
Leaves: They have antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and alkylamides, which have been used to boost the immune system and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall season.
Dry the plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks or with a desiccant for 1 week.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a still at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours or until 20-30 milliliters of oil is collected.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or centrifuge to remove any plant debris and impurities.
Store the echinacea purpurea essential oil in dark glass bottles sealed tightly with a dropper cap at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: Wintergreen oil's methyl salicylate content helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughing, sore throats, and congestion.
Expectorant properties: The volatile compounds in wintergreen oil help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieving stubborn coughs.

Antitussive properties: Wintergreen oil's menthol content helps to reduce the urge to cough by numbing the mucous membranes and reducing irritation in the throat, providing fast-acting relief from chronic coughing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, essential oils helps with cough because it contains methyl salicylate, a natural salicylate that mimics the pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties of aspirin. This makes it effective in reducing inflammation in the throat and airways, helping to alleviate coughing caused by respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The oil's decongestant properties also help to clear mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from coughs and congestion. Its antitussive properties also help to soothe the throat.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: This sesquiterpene acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax the airways and reduce coughing.
Bornyl formate: Another sesquiterpene, bornyl formate has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate coughing.
Gaultherin: A phenolic compound, gaultherin has expectorant properties, meaning it helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in methyl salicylate, a compound responsible for the oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps alleviate cough and sore throats.
Roots: Roots of Gaultheria procumbens contain a high concentration of methyl salicylate, similar to its leaves, making them a valuable source for essential oil production.
Stems: Stems of Gaultheria procumbens, although less commonly used, are also a source of essential oil with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 pounds of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems when they are in full growth.
Steam distill the plant material at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oils.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator funnel or a centrifuge.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the gaultheria procumbens essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil helps reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can alleviate a persistent cough caused by conditions like bronchitis or asthma.
Soothing and calming effect: The sedative properties of chamomile essential oil can help calm the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort that can trigger a cough.
Antispasmodic property: Chamomile essential oil's ability to relax muscles in the airways and throat helps relieve spasms that can cause a persistent cough, making it easier to breathe and sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, essential oils helps with cough because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's apigenin and chamazulene compounds have natural expectorant qualities that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways. This helps to reduce coughing spasms and makes it easier to breathe. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can help to alleviate stress and anxiety that can exacerbate coughing, promoting a more peaceful and restful recovery.
remedy_constituents: α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone acts as an anti-inflammatory and soothing agent, helping to calm the throat and reduce coughing.
α-Bisabolol acetate: Similar to α-bisabolol, it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate cough and sore throat.
Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relax the muscles in the throat and reduce coughing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most used part due to their high content of apigenin and bisabolol, which provide anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to soothe coughs.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their camomile leaves oil, which contains chamazulene, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and ease cough symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are occasionally used for their oil, which contains sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate cough discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dry flower heads at dawn or late afternoon to minimize oil content loss.
Dry the flower heads in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried flower heads at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours using a ratio of 1:5.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decanting it into a separate container within 30 minutes.
Store the resulting matricaria chamomilla essential oil in a dark glass bottle in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with cough because of its natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's camphor content helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, while its citral content has a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear out mucus from the airways. This combination allows lemongrass essential oils to effectively soothe and calm coughs, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Lemongrass essential oil has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which helps to combat infections that cause coughs.
Expectorant properties: The essential oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and coughing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can exacerbate cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemongrass roots help soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and relieving cough symptoms.
Leaves: Packed with citral and geranial, the leaves of lemongrass possess expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in sufficient quantity for processing.
Clean and wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Crush or grind the clean leaves into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Mix the crushed leaves with hexane at a ratio of 1:5 for solvent extraction, then stir well and cover container.
Allow the mixture to steep in a warm place for 24 hours, shaking occasionally, before filtering and separating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with cough because of its potent antimicrobial properties that combat respiratory infections, reducing congestion and inflammation. The oil's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve a persistent cough. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe irritated throats and airways, providing fast and effective relief from coughing fits. As a result, incorporating wormwood essential oils into your natural remedies can offer significant relief from chronic coughs and colds.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Wormwood essential oil has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against Streptococcus pneumoniae, which is responsible for many cases of pneumonia and respiratory tract infections that can lead to cough.
Expectorant property: Wormwood essential oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion-related coughs.

Anti-inflammatory property: Wormwood essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, which can contribute to a cough caused by respiratory infections or allergies.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for its bitter and stimulating properties, which help relieve respiratory issues such as coughs.
Barks: Rich in camphor and other terpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects to soothe coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves in amounts of 100g for steam distillation.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours.
Combine 50g of dried artemisia absinthium leaves with 500ml of distilled water in a large glass jar.
Steam distill the mixture using a clevenger apparatus or similar equipment for 2-3 hours to extract essential oils.
Separate the extracted essential oil from the distillate by decanting it into a dark glass bottle and storing it in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame essential oils helps with cough because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the irritated throat tissues. Its natural decongestant properties also ease congestion in the chest and sinuses, reducing coughing fits. Additionally, sesame oil's antimicrobial properties combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough. As a result, herbal sesame essential oils provide effective relief from persistent and nagging coughs, promoting easier breathing and a better night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sesame oil has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms such as chest congestion and coughing associated with respiratory issues.
Expectorant properties: The omega-6 fatty acids present in sesame oil have been found to help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and relieving coughs.

Antioxidant properties: Sesame oil contains antioxidants that can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to respiratory issues like coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesamin and sesamolin, these roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe a sore throat and relieve cough symptoms.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and omega-6 fatty acids, sesame seeds have expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sesamum indicum seeds and dry them thoroughly in the shade for at least seven days.
Crush dried sesamum indicum seeds into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Steam distill 100 grams of crushed sesamum indicum seeds in a still with 200 milliliters of water for six hours to obtain oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Bottle the filtered oil in dark glass bottles and store them in a cool place for future use after quality control checks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sarcococca essential oils helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The oil contains compounds such as limonene and pinene, which have been shown to ease respiratory congestion and reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs. When inhaled or applied topically, Sarcococca essential oils can help to break down mucus, soothe a sore throat, and calm coughing spasms, providing relief from persistent coughs and colds. remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Sarcococca essential oil has antitussive properties, which means it helps to relieve and calm coughing, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing dry or tickly throats.
Expectorant properties: The oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of a cough.

Antimicrobial properties: Sarcococca essential oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat bacterial or viral infections that may be contributing to a persistent cough, promoting faster recovery and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a soothing and expectorant oil that helps relieve cough symptoms.
Buds: The delicate buds of the Sarcococca plant contain concentrated amounts of aromatic oils that help ease cough congestion and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality t zeylanicus plant material by cutting fresh branches with a sterile knife.
Cleanly sort the harvested material into bundles of similar size and shape to ensure uniform steam flow.
Steam the plant material in a still at 100-110°c for 4-6 hours using a gentle flow rate to extract essential oils.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by allowing it to settle and then decanting the clear liquid into a clean container.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles, sealed tightly, and keep at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When inhaled, valerian oil reduces inflammation in the airways, providing instant relief from congestion and discomfort. Its calming effects also help to relax the throat muscles, reducing spasms that cause persistent coughing. Additionally, valerian's antimicrobial properties help combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses, further alleviating symptoms of respiratory illnesses like bronchitis and asthma. Overall, valerian essential oil provides a natural and effective remedy for soothing a nagging cough.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian essential oil helps to calm coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian essential oil relaxes the muscles in the respiratory tract, which can help to relieve coughs caused by spasms or irritation in the lungs and bronchi.
Expectorant properties: Valerian essential oil helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing the severity of a cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate cough symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a high concentration of essential oil, including sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antispasmodic and expectorant activities, making them effective against coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of valeriana officinalis at dawn or dusk for optimal quality and potency.
Cleanse the harvested roots by rinsing them under cold running water followed by gentle scrubbing with a soft-bristled brush.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight until their weight reduces by half over 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill 2 cups of dried valeriana officinalis roots with 4 cups of water at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours and 15 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles sealed tightly immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties. The oil's citrus flavor and aroma help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel. Additionally, bitter orange oil contains compounds like limonene and pinene, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce congestion and soothe an irritated throat. As a result, using herbal bitter orange essential oils in aromatherapy or topical applications may provide relief for coughs and colds.
remedy_properties: Antitussive property: Herbal bitter orange essential oil helps to soothe and calm a cough, reducing its severity and frequency.

Decongestant property: The oil's decongestant properties help to reduce the inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion associated with coughs.
Antioxidant property: Herbal bitter orange essential oil contains antioxidants that help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate respiratory issues like coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bitter orange plant contain compounds that help soothe and calm coughs, making them effective in treating dry, tickly coughs.
Barks: The barks of the bitter orange plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs, due to their antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bitter orange peels in early morning and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the peels using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or air-drying method for 7-10 days.
Transfer the dried peels into a clean glass jar and add 300ml of high-proof grain alcohol, then seal tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the orange essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Myrtle essential oils helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and hacking. The oil's antimicrobial compounds also help to combat underlying infections that can cause persistent coughing. Additionally, myrtle essential oil has a calming effect on the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and soothing irritations in the throat and lungs, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Myrtle essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and viral infections, which can cause coughs, by reducing their growth and replication.

Expectorant properties: Myrtle essential oil has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Myrtle essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to coughing and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Myrtle roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, making them effective in soothing irritated throats and relieving coughs.
Barks: Myrtle barks contain compounds that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it a popular ingredient in cough remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh leaves of myrtus communis at dawn or dusk for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly in cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a steam distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours to extract oil.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel for 30 minutes.
Transfer the collected essential oil into dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cough that are treated with herbal essential oils are bronchitis, sinusitis, and asthma. Eucalyptus oil is commonly used to relieve congestion and ease respiratory issues, while peppermint oil helps to reduce inflammation and open up airways. Thyme oil has antibacterial properties that aid in fighting infections, making it effective against sinusitis and bronchitis. These essential oils can be inhaled directly or added to diffusers for relief from these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal essential oils that help with cough is a blend of Eucalyptus, Peppermint, and Thyme. Eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties help relieve congestion, while Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and soothe the throat. Thyme oil's antimicrobial properties combat infection and promote healing. This synergy of oils can be diffused or added to a warm bath to provide quick relief from coughing and congestion, promoting a speedy recovery. Use 5-7 drops of each oil in your diffuser or bath for optimal results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with cough because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, help to break down and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up. This leads to quick relief from congestion and coughing. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing the severity of coughing fits and promoting a faster recovery from respiratory infections. This makes it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Helps reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving cough symptoms and making it easier to breathe.
Cineole: Exhibits expectorant properties, aiding in the removal of mucus from the lungs, thereby alleviating cough.
α-Pinene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and bronchodilator, helping to open up airways and reduce cough severity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Most commonly used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a primary component responsible for the oil's decongestant and expectorant properties.
Buds: Used in some blends to add a fresh and herbaceous note, as well as to provide additional eucalyptol content.
Barks: Utilized in certain traditional remedies and essential oil blends, as they contain a mix of eucalyptol and other compounds that contribute to the oil's therapeutic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature eucalyptus globulus leaves from the tops of the stems during the cooler parts of the day.
Dry the freshly harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves in a single layer with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill 50 kg of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a distillation apparatus using 20 kg of water for 3 hours.
Separate the resulting essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a paper filter to remove any impurities.
Store the final eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid away from heat and light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with cough because of its natural decongestant and expectorant properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from congestion and coughing. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, reducing discomfort and coughing fits. As a result, peppermint oil is often used in aromatherapy and herbal remedies to alleviate cough symptoms and promote respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps to thin mucus and relieve cough by creating a cooling sensation in the throat, making it easier to expel irritants.
Menthone: It has a similar effect to menthol, providing relief from cough by thinning mucus and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Limonene: It has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, providing relief from cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for making peppermint oil due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide a cooling effect and help alleviate cough symptoms.
Stems: Stems of Mentha x piperita are also used to produce essential oil, which contains a similar composition to the leaves, including menthol and menthone, making it effective in relieving cough.
Flowers: Peppermint flowers contain a higher concentration of menthone than leaves, making them a popular choice for producing essential oil to help soothe coughs and other respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning when the plant is at its peak potency.
Steam distill the harvested leaves and flowers in a still at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil by separating it from the water through a condenser and a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a candle filter or a paper filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with cough because its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help to combat the underlying infection causing the cough. The oil's immunomodulatory effects also support the body's natural defenses, allowing it to fight off the illness more effectively. Additionally, Echinacea has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughing and congestion. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate cough symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline Alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating cough symptoms.
Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds exhibit expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving cough symptoms.
Terpenes (specifically, Sesquiterpenes): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce the severity and duration of coughs caused by viral infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe a cough.
Leaves: They contain volatile compounds that can help to ease respiratory congestion and reduce coughing.
Flowers: They possess flavonoids and other compounds that have antitussive properties, helping to calm a cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers when they are fully open and in full bloom during summer months.
Combine the collected flowers with 4 cups of distilled water in a steam distillation apparatus.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove impurities.
Label and date the essential oil bottle and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with cough because it possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to combat viral and bacterial infections that cause coughing. By reducing inflammation and fighting off underlying infections, Thymus vulgaris essential oils can provide quick relief from coughing, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues. Its expectorant properties also help loosen mucus, promoting a smooth and easy cough.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps with cough by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, allowing for easier breathing and cough relief.
Thymol: It helps with cough by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough.
Carvacrol: It helps with cough by possessing expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of thymol, a compound that helps to relieve coughs and open airways.
Flowers: They have a calming effect and help to soothe the throat, reducing coughs and inflammation.
Stems: They contain a high amount of essential oils, which when distilled, can be used to create thyme oil, a natural remedy for coughs and respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at the peak of potency in early summer morning.
Steam distill the harvested material in a 2:1 water to herb ratio for 2-3 hours continuously.
Collect the distilled water and oil mixture and allow it to cool and separate for 30 minutes naturally.
Strain the oil from the water using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities slowly.
Store the extracted thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from heat and light sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat. The expectorant properties of licorice root oil help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion. Additionally, its antitussive properties help to suppress the cough reflex, providing temporary relief from coughing. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils can provide fast and effective relief from coughs.
remedy_constituents: Liquiritigenin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and anti-cough agent by reducing inflammation in the airways and suppressing cough reflex.
Licoricidin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thus alleviating cough symptoms.
Glycyrrhizin: Functions as an anti-inflammatory and anti-irritant, soothing the throat and reducing inflammation, which helps to calm and alleviate coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils for cough because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils for cough because they exhibit expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and coughs.

(Optional) Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils for cough because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe the throat and relieve coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-75 kg of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from fields with optimal sunlight and moisture levels.
Steam distill the roots at 100-110°c for 4-6 hours to release the essential oil from the plant.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separator to separate the oil from water and other impurities.
Filter the essential oil through a charcoal filter to remove any remaining impurities and improve its quality.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve its potency and aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the respiratory system. The oil, commonly known as ginger essential oil, contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion. By loosening mucus and reducing inflammation in the throat and lungs, ginger essential oil can provide quick relief from coughs and colds, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to ease the discomfort associated with coughing.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: It helps reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby easing cough.
Gingerols: They have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, which in turn relieves cough.
Shogaols: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, reducing the severity of cough caused by viral infections.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems are used to make essential oils because they contain high concentrations of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used to make essential oils because they are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate cough and respiratory issues.
Leaves: The leaves of Zingiber officinale are used to make essential oils because they contain volatile oils with decongestant and expectorant properties, which help relieve cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from a mature plant at dawn when the dew is still present.
Wash the rhizomes gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris then dry them thoroughly.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes in a still at 100-150 degrees celsius for 4-6 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the still and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with cough because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the respiratory tract. The oil's antimicrobial action also helps to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the cough. Additionally, its decongestant properties help to ease congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits. This makes it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughing.
Bornyl acetate: It has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion.
Bornel: It has a calming effect on the respiratory system, reducing irritation and inflammation, and helping to soothe coughs.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Rosmarinus officinalis to make essential oils for cough due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are also used to make essential oils for cough, as they contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are sometimes used to make essential oils for cough, as they contain rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds that have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers from the plant at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested leaves and flowers for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled water and essential oil and allow it to settle for 1-2 hours.
Decant the clear essential oil from the top layer into a clean glass container for storage.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It soothes the throat and calms the respiratory system, reducing irritation and congestion. The oil's expectorant properties also help to loosen mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia's calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate coughing. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to alleviate coughs and promote a peaceful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby reducing cough symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, leading to a decrease in coughing.
Caryophyllene oxide: Demonstrates bronchodilatory effects, causing the airways to dilate and relax, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughs.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most widely used part for making essential oils due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have calming and decongestant properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain a smaller amount of essential oils, but they still contribute to the overall oil's antispasmodic and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughs.

Stems: The stems of the plant contain a significant amount of essential oils, which are often used to create a more cost-effective alternative to the flowers, while still providing some cough-relieving benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of dried flowers at peak bloom in the morning to ensure maximum oil content.
Dry the flowers in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried flowers at 100-110°c using a distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a condenser and separator equipment.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens essential oils helps with cough because of its soothing and expectorant properties. The oil contains compounds like camphor, linalool, and pinene, which have decongestant and bronchodilator effects, helping to relieve congestion and open up airways. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to cough up mucus and phlegm. This natural remedy can provide relief from coughs, making it a popular choice for those looking for a holistic approach to managing respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce cough by its antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which can ease the spasms in the respiratory tract and promote the expulsion of mucus.
Geranial: Exhibits expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, thereby relieving cough.
Caryophyllene oxide: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating cough caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oil, which is used to treat cough due to their antitussive and expectorant properties.
Stems: Stems of Hyptis suaveolens are also used to produce essential oil, which helps in relieving cough symptoms.
Flowers: Flowers of the plant are used to make essential oil, which is known for its antitussive and anti-inflammatory properties to treat cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of fresh hyptis suaveolens leaves and flowers at dawn when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested material in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried material using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100-110°c for 4-6 hours.
Collect the essential oil that condenses in the condenser coil and separate it from the water phase.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pimpinella anisum essential oils helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The aniseed oil's sweet, licorice-like flavor and aroma also have a soothing effect on the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation. By promoting the removal of excess mucus and providing a calming sensation, Pimpinella anisum essential oils can provide relief from coughs and congestion, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues. Its effectiveness is attributed to its unique composition of compounds.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Acts as an expectorant to relieve cough by thinning mucus and facilitating its expulsion from the respiratory tract.
Eugenol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the throat and airways, which can help alleviate cough caused by irritation.
Linalool: Possesses antitussive properties, directly inhibiting the cough reflex and providing relief from coughing spasms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Pimpinella anisum are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of anise oil, which has been traditionally used to treat cough and other respiratory issues.
Leaves: The leaves of Pimpinella anisum are used to make essential oils as they contain compounds with expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve cough and congestion.
Flowers: The flowers of Pimpinella anisum are used to make essential oils due to their sweet, anise-like fragrance and their ability to soothe respiratory issues, including cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the pimpinella anisum flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom for optimal oil production.
Steam distill the harvested flowers and leaves for 3-4 hours at 100-110 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter or a coffee filter.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight.
Label the bottle with the date and the name pimpinella anisum essential oil for future reference.
